steps.title: Identify the defining action in the sentence
steps.content:
The sentence says OSIRIS-REx "successfully ______ a sample of the surface, gathering pieces of it to bring back to Earth." The phrase "gathering pieces of it to bring back" is a participial modifier that describes what the spacecraft did to the sample. The blank must be a verb that means acquiring or taking material from the surface.
  • Choice A — 'attached': Means fastened or joined — the spacecraft was not fastening itself to a sample. ✗
  • Choice B — 'collected': Means gathered or acquired — directly matches "gathering pieces of it to bring back to Earth." ✓
  • Choice C — 'followed': Means went after — unrelated to sampling. ✗
  • Choice D — 'replaced': Means substituted — implies removing and putting something else in its place, not gathering a sample. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the best fit
steps.content: The participial phrase immediately after the blank ("gathering pieces of it to bring back to Earth") is the key: it restates the blank's meaning in plain language. Only "collected" aligns with gathering material for return transport. That matches choice B — B is correct.
